model_assessment.failure_mode: New York failed to separate after halftime; Portland three-point variance and bench scoring held, while NY turnover count and defensive leakage triggered margin compression collapse.

halftime_snapshot.entry_note: User locked NYL -12.5 at -112, within max -125 price cap. Classified as yellow micro-position.

NYL.B_Stewart: 16 pts, 10 reb, 4 ast, 3 blk; scoring normalized somewhat after slow start but not enough for separation.
NYL.J_Jones: 17 pts, 7 reb, 3 ast, 4 TOV; efficient scoring but turnover drag.
NYL.M_Johannes: 18 pts, 4 reb, 4 ast; perimeter scoring stable.
NYL.P_Astier: 24 pts, 5 reb, 3 ast, 5 TOV; offensive engine but turnover pressure contributed to margin failure.

PDX.B_Carleton: 26 pts, 5 threes, 4 steals; primary variance spike and defensive disruption source.
PDX.C_Leite: 21 pts, 4 reb, 6 ast; lead guard control and late-game stability.
PDX.K_Smalls: 13 pts off bench on 4-5 3PT; bench shooting spike broke NY spread extension thesis.
PDX.L_Geiselsoder: 13 pts, 5 reb, 3 threes; frontcourt spacing sustained PDX offense.

rebound_environment.summary: NYL won total rebounds 31-26, but offensive rebounds were even at 8-8. Rebounding did not create separation for NY; PDX matched second-chance output 10-9 and neutralized NY's board advantage.
notes: NY had a 23-18 defensive rebounding edge but could not convert into margin extension.
Even OREB count prevented NY from generating a possession cushion.
PDX second-chance scoring and perimeter efficiency offset NY rebounding advantage.
